name: Aisha

profile.shopping_behaviour: Hunts Depop while impulse buying from SHEIN, sends lowball offers on secondhand items. Discovers on TikTok, buys on resale apps, occasionally splurges on Brandy Melville basics.
profile.personality: Torn between sustainability and micro-trends on budget. Enjoys handwritten package notes and Depop seller drama. Frustrated fast fashion outsells vintage cotton despite knowing she's complicit.
profile.style_identity: Chases Y2K viral pieces: heart necklaces, grommet belts, Brandy sweats. Seeks durable 100% cotton basics at student prices. Owns excessive skirts for outfit content creation.

preview_description: Leah embodies Gen Z's sustainability paradox: she searches Depop and Vinted for secondhand Brandy Melville and Zara but impulse-buys from SHEIN when frustrated, guilt-ridden over environmental impact yet addicted to TikTok micro-trends. She loves Depop's chaotic theatre but settles for fraying polyester basics instead of quality pieces.

preview_attributes.key_brands: Depop, SHEIN, Brandy Melville, Zara, H&M, Vinted
preview_attributes.style_identity: TikTok-driven Y2K revival with sustainability guilt
preview_attributes.shopping_triggers: Viral trends, FOMO, seeing styled outfit inspo, good Depop banter
preview_attributes.pain_points: Everything affordable is polyester, quality-price disconnect, seller flakiness, knowing fast fashion is bad but needing cheap trendy pieces
